calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Aug-06-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jonathan Kim of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Triple A Machine Shop, Inc.

RULING: MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL
Not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Douglas G. Wah and Arturo E. Sandoval on behalf of Foley & Mansfield, PLLP's motion to be relieved as counsel for defendant Triple A Machine Shop, Inc. is GRANTED. Notice of Association of Counsel adding Hawkins Parnell & Young, LLP filed on June 22, 2020. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition Of Karen Hall To Perpetuate Testimony Of Jesse J. Hall By Videotaped Deposition
Off Calendar Sep-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jordan Scott of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for petitioner / moving party Karen Hall
Cleve B. Collado of YARON & ASSOCIATES (415-658-2929), appearing for respondent / opposing party Puget Sound Commerce Center, Inc. FKA Todd Shipyards Corporation
Arturo E. Sandoval of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for respondent / opposing party Triple A Machine Shop, Inc.
Nicole E. Gage of TUCKER ELLIS LLP (415-617-2105), specially appearing for respondent / opposing party Carrier Corporation
Charles S. Bishop of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), specially appearing for respondent Mine Safety Appliances Company, LLC
Alexander J. Alpert of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), specially appearing for respondent Woodward
Mitchell B. Greenberg of ABBEY, WEITZENBERG, WARREN & EMERY (707-542-5050), specially appearing via CourtCall for Anchor Darling Valve Company

RULING:
KAREN HALL'S PETITION TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY OF JESSE J. HALL BY VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Karen Hall's petition to perpetuate testimony of Jesse J. Hall by videotaped deposition is granted in part and denied in part. The petition is denied as to Crane Co. as there are is no proof of service on file demonstrating that it was served with the petition and notice of the hearing. The petition is granted as unopposed as to J & H Marine & Industrial Engineering Company, Flowserve US Inc., Mine Safety Appliances Company, LLC, Anchor Darling Valve Company, ABB Inc., and Woodward Governor Company. Petitioner has dismissed Electrolux Home Products, Inc. The petition is granted as to the remaining entities identified in paragraph 11 because the petition meets the requirements of C.C.P. section 2035.030(b) and does not violate section 2035.010(b). The petition demonstrates that the discovery requested may prevent a failure or delay of justice. (C.C.P. 2035.050(a).) The deposition may take place at or near Mr. Hall's home in Fairfield, CA. It shall be scheduled for a date at least 20 days after the September 5, 2017 hearing date. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)
